issues.title: Add a builder for user resource-sharing request bodies (build_user_rs_request)

tests.scope: Network-free proof, run against the shipped build the operator will actually install, that build_user_rs_request encodes Alma's plain-vs-{'value': ...} asymmetry correctly. Exercises: the package-root export (from almaapitk import build_user_rs_request); the wrapping asymmetry that is the whole point of issue #197 (owner plain, format/citation_type/pickup_location wrapped, pickup_location_type plain); plain-string handling of title/journal_title/author/external_id and int->str coercion of year; agree_to_copyright_terms defaulting to a real bool True; the 'extra' escape hatch (a plain string given for a wrapped field is wrapped, an already-wrapped dict passes through untouched, a plain field stays plain); the required-argument guards on owner/format/citation_type; and — via inspect — that Users.create_user_rs_request still takes request_data as a required positional (the raw-dict path is untouched) with validate as a keyword-only kwarg defaulting to False. The 'encoded exactly once' claim is checked structurally: both the builder and the extra-merge path resolve wrapping through the single module-level frozenset _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS, so membership of that one set is asserted rather than each call site. The builder is a pure function: no SANDBOX state is read or mutated and no HTTP request is issued, which is exactly why this test carries no fixtures and no endpoints. The bulk of #197's behaviour is already pinned by tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_197.py and tests/unit/domains/test_users.py; this test exists only to confirm those guarantees survive into the installed artifact.
pythonCalls:
body = build_user_rs_request(
    owner='<RS_LIB>',
    format='DIGITAL',
    citation_type='CR',
    title='AlmaAPITK chunk-test RS builder',
    journal_title='AlmaAPITK test journal',
    author='AlmaAPITK',
    year=2026,
    pickup_location='<PICKUP_LIB>',
    pickup_location_type='LIBRARY',
    agree_to_copyright_terms=True,
    external_id='almaapitk-chunk-test-197',
)

plain_text_ok = (
    body['title'] == 'AlmaAPITK chunk-test RS builder'
    and body['journal_title'] == 'AlmaAPITK test journal'
    and body['author'] == 'AlmaAPITK'
    and body['external_id'] == 'almaapitk-chunk-test-197'
    and body['year'] == '2026'
)

single_source_ok = (
    {'format', 'citation_type', 'pickup_location'} <= _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS
    and 'owner' not in _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS
    and 'pickup_location_type' not in _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS
    and 'external_id' not in _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS
)

extra_body = build_user_rs_request(
    owner='<RS_LIB>',
    format='PHYSICAL',
    citation_type='BK',
    title='AlmaAPITK chunk-test extra escape hatch',
    extra={
        'partner': 'ILL_PARTNER',
        'pickup_location': {'value': '<PICKUP_LIB>'},
        'note': 'plain note',
    },
)

raw_dict_path_ok = (
    'request_data' in sig.parameters
    and sig.parameters['request_data'].default is inspect.Parameter.empty
)

builder_is_opt_in = (
    sig.parameters['validate'].default is False
    and sig.parameters['validate'].kind is inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY
)
passCriteria: root_export_ok is True — build_user_rs_request is importable from the package root (from almaapitk import build_user_rs_request) and callable
owner_plain is True — owner is emitted as a plain string, never wrapped in {'value': ...}
wrapped_ok is True — format, citation_type and pickup_location are each emitted as {'value': <code>}
pickup_type_plain is True — pickup_location_type is emitted as a plain string
plain_text_ok is True — title, journal_title, author and external_id are plain strings, and an int year is coerced to the string '2026'
copyright_ok is True — agree_to_copyright_terms defaults to the bool True (not the string 'True')

single_source_ok is True — the wrapped-field set _RS_REQUEST_WRAPPED_FIELDS contains format/citation_type/pickup_location and excludes owner/pickup_location_type/external_id, i.e. the wrapping rule has exactly one definition site
extra_wraps is True — a plain string passed through extra for a wrapped field is wrapped
extra_passthrough is True — an already-wrapped dict passed through extra is not double-wrapped
extra_plain is True — a plain field passed through extra is left plain
guard_errors has exactly the keys 'owner', 'format' and 'citation_type', and each message names the field it rejected
raw_dict_path_ok is True — Users.create_user_rs_request still accepts request_data as a required positional argument, so the raw-dict path is unchanged
builder_is_opt_in is True — validate is keyword-only and defaults to False, so neither the builder nor the new validation changes existing caller behaviour
No HTTP request is issued by this test (endpoints is empty) and no SANDBOX state is read or mutated

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live round-trip: the one genuinely live-worthy check for #197 — that a body produced entirely by build_user_rs_request is ACCEPTED BY ALMA on a real create. Only Alma can confirm the emitted shape (owner plain, format/citation_type/pickup_location wrapped, pickup_location_type plain) is the shape Alma wants. Code values amended 2026-07-22 after live SB evidence: format DIGITAL + citation_type CR (a scanned-article request). The previous prescription (PHYSICAL + BOOK) reproducibly dies with a raw HTTP 500 in SB regardless of owner/pickup/external_id (BOOK is a purchase-request table code), while DIGITAL+CR creates cleanly AND persists+enriches the citation (verified A/B pair 2026-07-22). DIGITAL articles make journal_title+year+author mandatory (alma_code 401930), so both are supplied. Sequence: build -> create -> get (confirm request_id round-trips). Per standing operator instruction (2026-07-22) the created request is NOT cancelled: it is GET-verified and left in place, its id printed as 'LEFT-IN-PLACE request_id=<id>'.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read/write resource-sharing-request smoke testing. The user MUST be affiliated with a resource-sharing library (Alma error 401768 otherwise). Used by t-197-2 (live create -> get -> cancel of a builder-produced body) and t-194-2 (deliberately-rejected wrong-code create). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

The Alma resource-sharing library code that ${existing_user_primary_id} is affiliated with — the 'owner' field of the resource-sharing request body. Required by Alma per error code 401607 ('Resource sharing library (owner) is missing'). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

The Alma library code to use as the pickup location for the resource-sharing request (the 'pickup_location' field, sent wrapped alongside pickup_location_type='LIBRARY'). Alma returns error 401894 ('Missing mandatory field: Pickup location library code') when it is absent. May be the same code as ${rs_library_code} if the RS library also accepts pickups. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

pythonCalls:
built_body = None
create_response = None
create_data = None
request_id = None
get_response = None
get_request_id_echo = None
external_id_echo = None
cancel_response = None
raised_error = None

built_body = build_user_rs_request(
    owner='${rs_library_code}',
    format='DIGITAL',
    citation_type='CR',
    title='AlmaAPITK chunk-test RS request (issue 197 builder)',
    journal_title='AlmaAPITK Chunk Test Journal',
    author='AlmaAPITK',
    year=2026,
    pickup_location='${pickup_library_code}',
    pickup_location_type='LIBRARY',
    agree_to_copyright_terms=True,
    external_id='almaapitk-chunk-test-197',
)

print(
    '[t-197-2] built body: keys=%s owner_is_plain=%s format=%s pickup_location_type=%s'
    % (
        sorted(built_body),
        isinstance(built_body['owner'], str),
        built_body['format'],
        built_body['pickup_location_type'],
    )
)

create_data = getattr(create_response, 'data', None)
if isinstance(create_data, dict):
    request_id = create_data.get('request_id') or create_data.get('id')
print(f'[t-197-2] RS request created: request_id={request_id!r}')

get_response = users.get_user_rs_request('${existing_user_primary_id}', str(request_id)) if request_id else None
if isinstance(get_response, dict):
    get_request_id_echo = get_response.get('request_id') or get_response.get('id')
    external_id_echo = get_response.get('external_id')
print(f'[t-197-2] external_id echoed back by Alma: {external_id_echo!r} (informational only)')
passCriteria: create_response is not None and getattr(create_response, 'success', False) is True — Alma ACCEPTED a request body produced entirely by build_user_rs_request
No AlmaAPIError was raised on the create; in particular Alma did not return the cryptic 'Invalid field value ... Value: {1}' rejection that issue #197 exists to prevent
isinstance(create_data, dict) and request_id is not None — Alma returned a request_id for the created request
isinstance(get_response, dict) and str(get_request_id_echo) == str(request_id) — the created request is retrievable and round-trips its identifier
No '[almaapitk hint:' text appears anywhere in this test's output — the issue-194 code-table hint must not fire on a valid call

The created request is deliberately NOT cancelled (standing operator instruction 2026-07-22): a 'LEFT-IN-PLACE request_id=' marker line is printed so the operator can inspect the request in Alma SANDBOX

issues.title: create_user_rs_request: no guardrail against wrong code-table values → cryptic Alma error

tests.scope: Network-free proof that the opt-in soft validation (issue #194 option 2) is wired into the public Users.create_user_rs_request path in the shipped build and fires BEFORE any HTTP request. Exercises: a wrong-table format code ('P', the purchase-request code that is the issue's reproduction case) raising AlmaValidationError that NAMES the field — the entire point, since Alma's own rejection names nothing; the same for pickup_location_type and citation_type; the deliberate permissiveness on citation_type (all four of BK, CR, BOOK, JOURNAL pass, because the XSD, the docs and the empirically-passing SANDBOX request disagree and the shipped code refuses to pick a side); acceptance of both encodings a caller may have used ({'value': '<code>'} wrapper and bare string), since create_user_rs_request forwards request_data verbatim; skipping (rather than rejecting) values that are not plain strings; validate being keyword-only with default False so tenants with extended code tables are unaffected; and the docstring carrying the purchase-vs-lending-vs-borrowing code cheat sheet (issue #194 option 3). The user id used is the literal placeholder '<user_primary_id>' which does not exist in SANDBOX — it passes the wrapper's non-empty-string guard but nothing more, so if validation ever FAILED to short-circuit the call, the leaked network request would surface as an AlmaAPIError instead of the expected AlmaValidationError and the test would fail loudly. That is deliberate: it is what makes 'before the network call' observable without a fixture. No SANDBOX state is read or mutated. The pass-direction assertions call the module-level pure helper _validate_rs_borrowing_codes directly, because driving them through the public method with a valid body would issue a real POST — which is exactly what this network-free test must not do.
pythonCalls:
sig = inspect.signature(Users.create_user_rs_request)
validate_default_off = (
    sig.parameters['validate'].default is False
    and sig.parameters['validate'].kind is inspect.Parameter.KEYWORD_ONLY
)

doc = Users.create_user_rs_request.__doc__ or ''
cheatsheet_ok = (
    'PHYSICAL' in doc
    and 'DIGITAL' in doc
    and 'purchase' in doc.lower()
    and 'lending' in doc.lower()
)

print(f'[t-194-1] format_error_raised={format_error is not None} pickup_type_error_raised={pickup_type_error is not None} citation_error_raised={citation_error is not None} validate_default_off={validate_default_off}')
passCriteria: format_error is not None — an AlmaValidationError (not an AlmaAPIError) was raised, proving the check ran before any HTTP request left the process
format_error contains the field name 'format' and the rejected value 'P' — the guardrail names the offending field, which Alma's own error never does
format_error lists the expected codes: it contains both 'PHYSICAL' and 'DIGITAL'
pickup_type_error is not None and contains 'pickup_location_type'
citation_error is not None and contains 'citation_type'
permissive_ok is True — all four of BK, CR, BOOK and JOURNAL are accepted for citation_type; the shipped check does not pick a side in the XSD-vs-empirical disagreement
bare_string_ok is True and bare_string_rejects_bad_code is True — both the {'value': ...} wrapper and a bare string are understood, since request_data is forwarded verbatim
skip_nonstring_ok is True — a value that is not a plain string is skipped rather than rejected
unchecked_fields_ok is True — only format, citation_type and pickup_location_type are inspected; nothing else about the body is validated or normalised
validate_default_off is True — validate is keyword-only and defaults to False, so tenants with extended code tables are opted out by default
cheatsheet_ok is True — the create_user_rs_request docstring carries the borrowing code values and references the purchase and lending surfaces
No HTTP request is issued by this test (endpoints is empty) and no SANDBOX state is read or mutated

tests.scope: Operator-authorized live reproduction of the issue: deliberately POST a wrong-code-table value to the RS-borrowing endpoint and confirm the client turns Alma's dead-end error into an actionable one. This is the one part of #194 that unit tests cannot settle, because it depends on Alma actually still emitting the mangled 'Invalid field value. Field: [Ljava.lang.Object;@..., Value: {1}' message — the client only appends its hint when it detects that exact shape. The payload is byte-for-byte the known-good body from the previous chunk's passing live test EXCEPT format, which is set to the purchase-request code 'P' (the issue's exact reproduction). validate= is deliberately NOT passed, which does double duty: it proves validation is off by default (the request reaches Alma at all) and it lets the error-hint path run. Expected outcome: Alma rejects the create with HTTP 400, the wrapper raises AlmaAPIError, and the message carries an appended '[almaapitk hint: ...]' naming format/PHYSICAL/DIGITAL and pointing at build_user_rs_request and validate=True. Because the create is rejected, no request should be created — but this test still issues a POST to a mutating endpoint, so it is declared stateChanging with a defensive cleanup that cancels anything Alma unexpectedly accepted. IF THE REPRODUCTION NO LONGER REPRODUCES: should Alma have fixed its message-template rendering upstream so the error now names the field, 'mangled_shape' will be False and the client will (correctly, by design) append no hint. That is an upstream environment change, not a defect in this chunk — record it in the results and treat the hint criteria as not-applicable; the hint logic itself is pinned unconditionally by t-194-3 and by tests/unit/regressions/test_issue_194.py. Prerequisites are the same as t-197-2: the test user must be affiliated with the resource-sharing library supplied as ${rs_library_code}.

needsHumanInput.description: An existing Alma user_primary_id in SANDBOX that the operator has authorized for read/write resource-sharing-request smoke testing. The user MUST be affiliated with a resource-sharing library (Alma error 401768 otherwise). Used by t-197-2 (live create -> get -> cancel of a builder-produced body) and t-194-2 (deliberately-rejected wrong-code create). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real user_primary_id into this file.

The Alma resource-sharing library code that ${existing_user_primary_id} is affiliated with — the 'owner' field of the resource-sharing request body. Required by Alma per error code 401607 ('Resource sharing library (owner) is missing'). Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

The Alma library code to use as the pickup location for the resource-sharing request (the 'pickup_location' field, sent wrapped alongside pickup_location_type='LIBRARY'). Alma returns error 401894 ('Missing mandatory field: Pickup location library code') when it is absent. May be the same code as ${rs_library_code} if the RS library also accepts pickups. Synthetic placeholder only — the SANDBOX-test runner substitutes a real value at runtime; never write a real library code into this file.

pythonCalls:
raised = None
unexpected_response = None
unexpected_request_id = None
message = ''
print('[t-194-2] posting a deliberately wrong format code (P = purchase-request table) with validate= NOT passed, to provoke Alma mangled error')
message = str(raised) if raised is not None else ''
hint_names_format = "'format'" in message and 'PHYSICAL/DIGITAL' in message
hint_points_at_helpers = 'build_user_rs_request' in message and 'validate=True' in message
raw_dict_reached_alma = raised is not None or unexpected_response is not None
print(f'[t-194-2] raised={type(raised).__name__ if raised is not None else None} mangled_shape={mangled_shape} hint_present={hint_present} unexpected_request_id={unexpected_request_id!r}')
passCriteria: raised is not None and isinstance(raised, AlmaAPIError) — Alma rejected the wrong-code body
unexpected_response is None and unexpected_request_id is None — no resource-sharing request was created
raw_dict_reached_alma is True — the hand-assembled raw dict was forwarded to Alma verbatim and the request left the process, proving validation is OFF by default (validate= was not passed)
mangled_shape is True — Alma still returns its 'Invalid field value ...' message, i.e. the issue-194 reproduction still reproduces in SANDBOX (see the scope note if this is False)
hint_present is True — the client appended an '[almaapitk hint: ...]' suffix to Alma's un-actionable message
hint_names_format is True — the hint names 'format' and its expected PHYSICAL/DIGITAL values
hint_points_at_helpers is True — the hint points the caller at build_user_rs_request and validate=True
not_a_pol_error is True — the exception is not AlmaInvalidPolModeError; the acquisitions meaning of a colliding error code must not leak onto the RS surface
hasattr(raised, 'tracking_id') is True — the hint is message-only; the exception's attributes are unchanged
cleanup:
# The create is EXPECTED to be rejected, so normally there is nothing
# to clean up. This block exists because the test issues a POST to a
# mutating endpoint: if Alma unexpectedly ACCEPTED the wrong-code body,
# cancel the resulting request immediately so no live SANDBOX state is
# left behind. Do NOT re-raise — let the original failure surface.
try:
    if unexpected_request_id:
        _users_cleanup = _UsersCleanup(client)
        _users_cleanup.cancel_user_rs_request(
            '${existing_user_primary_id}',
            str(unexpected_request_id),
            reason='AUTOMATED_REGRESSION_TEST_CLEANUP',
            notify_user=False,
        )
        print(f'[t-194-2 cleanup] cancelled unexpectedly-created request_id={unexpected_request_id!r}')
    else:
        print('[t-194-2 cleanup] nothing to clean up: Alma rejected the wrong-code create, as expected')
except AlmaAPIError as cleanup_err:
    print(
        '\n!!! [t-194-2] MANUAL CLEANUP REQUIRED !!!'
        f'\n!!! request_id: {unexpected_request_id!r}'
        '\n!!! user: ${existing_user_primary_id}   RS library (owner): ${rs_library_code}'
        f'\n!!! reason: {cleanup_err}'
        "\n!!! Cancel the leftover RS request via the Alma staff UI (title starts 'AlmaAPITK chunk-test').\n"
    )

tests.scope: Network-free proof, against the shipped build, of the two client-side error-surfacing behaviours that t-194-2 can only sample once: (a) the mangled-message detector and its endpoint-scoped hint selection, and (b) the error-code classification changes. Exercises: the RS-borrowing hint is chosen when the failing URL is on the resource-sharing surface; the generic hint is chosen elsewhere; a well-rendered Alma 'Invalid field value' message that DOES name its field is left completely untouched (no hint — the additive change must not degrade already-actionable errors); an unrelated error message is returned unchanged; the 40166411 collision is resolved endpoint-scoped, so the code keeps its acquisitions AlmaInvalidPolModeError meaning by default but does NOT produce a misleading POL-mode exception on the RS surface; and 401890 ('User with identifier X of type Y was not found', delivered as HTTP 400 so the 404 status fallback never fires) is mapped to AlmaResourceNotFoundError like its near-twin 401861. All calls are pure functions over strings — no HTTP request is issued and no SANDBOX state is read or mutated.
pythonCalls:
from almaapitk.client.AlmaAPIClient import (
    ERROR_CODE_REGISTRY,
    AlmaAPIError,
    AlmaInvalidPolModeError,
    AlmaResourceNotFoundError,
    _augment_code_table_error_message,
)

well_rendered = 'Invalid field value. Field: format, Value: P'
untouched = _augment_code_table_error_message(well_rendered, rs_url)

unrelated_in = 'User with identifier X of type Y was not found.'
unrelated_out = _augment_code_table_error_message(unrelated_in, rs_url)
passCriteria: rs_hinted starts with the original mangled message and contains '[almaapitk hint:', 'PHYSICAL/DIGITAL', 'BK/CR', 'build_user_rs_request' and 'validate=True'
generic_hinted contains '[almaapitk hint:' but does NOT contain 'PHYSICAL/DIGITAL' — the hint is endpoint-scoped, not one-size-fits-all
untouched == well_rendered — an Alma error that DID name its field gets no hint appended (purely additive, never degrading)
unrelated_out == unrelated_in — messages that are not the mangled 'Invalid field value' shape are returned unchanged
pol_default is AlmaInvalidPolModeError — off the RS surface, error code 40166411 keeps its acquisitions meaning (no regression)
pol_on_rs is AlmaAPIError and pol_on_rs is not AlmaInvalidPolModeError — on the RS surface the collision is resolved so a bad parameter is not reported as a POL-mode error
user_not_found is AlmaResourceNotFoundError and registry_401890 is AlmaResourceNotFoundError — 401890 is mapped despite arriving as HTTP 400
registry_40166411 is AlmaInvalidPolModeError — the registry default is unchanged; only the endpoint-scoped override differs
No HTTP request is issued by this test (endpoints is empty) and no SANDBOX state is read or mutated

unmappable.ac: AC-194-9 (from the issue's 'Why this is easy to hit' table): settle which citation_type codes the borrowing surface actually accepts — the borrowing XSD and docs/Alma Borrowing Request API Guide.md say BK/CR, while the passing SANDBOX request in chunks/users-requests-followup/sandbox-tests/test_t_42_3.py used BOOK, and the lending/purchase surfaces use BOOK/JOURNAL.
unmappable.reason: Determining Alma's real accepted set would take one state-changing live create per candidate code (four creates plus four cancels, each leaving an audit artifact on the test user), and the answer would still not generalise: Alma code tables are tenant-extensible, so a SANDBOX result is evidence about this institution's configuration only — which is precisely why the shipped check is opt-in and accepts all four codes rather than picking a side. Testing it here would produce a library-level guarantee the library cannot make. Recommend resolving with Ex Libris support (or by inspecting the tenant's own Citation Type code table in the Alma config UI) and then narrowing _RS_BORROWING_CITATION_TYPE_CODES in a follow-up issue, rather than probing SANDBOX.
